It's a blessing to be amongst people I used to idolise. 

Hopefully we can keep living up to expectations and freeing everyone from the trenches. 

#apestogetherstrong 🦧

> @Frenchie_ French buy-side specialist. And a goat for getting us together.  

> @michaelsikand US market analyst and commentator

> @Kaizen_Investor Belgian AI hardware analyst  

> @damnang2 Engineer and silicon expert  

> @ParadisLab PE/IB analyst and thematic investor in hedge funds  

> @PhotonCap Korean semiconductor expert  

> @aleabitoreddit The famous "Did you listen Anon?" and the team coach

$UMAC Unusual Machines, $RCAT Red Cat Holdings and $LASE Laser Photonics continue to ride the US drone manufacturing policy push.

The Trump administration's program to fund US drone makers (cutting reliance on Chinese DJI components) is now extending into defense procurement. Laser Photonics' anti-drone system was selected by the US Department of War earlier this month.

Yeah France has a good squad but have you heard of the bottleneck bros?

Unbeatable team ⚡️ 

> @Frenchie_ French buy-side specialist. And a goat for getting us together.

text:
Market Review 20260623
- Total Carnage! What a rug pull. 
- The good news: this is not taking us by surprise. Whilst we took some paper cuts in the past 2 weeks, we've largely avoided getting caught in a big drawdown by holding a disproportionate amount of cash coming into this. 
- You may be wondering, why even have any paper cuts (small losses) at all? Because we CANNOT predict anything. We do not know WITH CERTAINTY whether the market will Up, Down or Sideways tomorrow or any time in the future. That means when we get good setups, we take the trade. But we manage our risk TIGHTLY at the same time so that if it turns out wrong, we don't lose a lot of money. But when it goes our way, we let it ride a little (eg our picks in biotech). 
- I will make a separate post about ZETA (which we bought today, in the midst of the selloff) as it will illustrate my thinking in more detail. 
- Guess what, technically the indices are still in an uptrend! But as I've said previously, the easy money is over. Even if the trend keeps going, it will be very volatile. I am a little encouraged that whilst the SPX is down 1.44% today, the equal weighted RSP is only down 0.34%. This tells me a lot of the selling has been led by mega caps. Which is no wonder. Remember our post yesterday? We talked about how the MAG7 is totally thrashed for now. 
- Similar picture, but more nasty in the Nasdaq, which is down 3.3% for the day. See the post last week on 17 Jun 2026 where I gave a high level overview of the key tech sectors like Semicon, Photonics etc.  I found it hard to get heavily involved in most of the AI Hardware related names. And the market is "funny/ironic" like this. I suspect A LOT of latecomers and retail will be rushing into the AI hardware trade / or had rushed in a few weeks ago on the dip. Why? Because they were locked out since the 30 Mar bottom. And the first chance they get to buy on a pullback 1-2 weeks ago, a lot may have rushed in. Why ironic? Because this has usually been how the market worked. Sucker in a lot of latecomers at the top. 
- How do you prevent something like this? Situational Awareness. I can't do all this in one post, and there is a lot of nuance involved. Experience and having seen many market turns in the past counts too. 
- Right now, I am still NOT prepared to call this a market top. The trend is still up, although there is a very high chance we will at least go into choppy sideways action for a while. The SPX has now come up against its 50SMA support, and we have earnings season soon. Maybe that will keep the market supported. On the bear side, the SPX higher timeframe (Weekly) MOMENTUM is threatening to cut / has nicked Down. This will imply the current lower timeframe (Daily) has turned DOWN.
- Nothing much to say about BTC and Gold, both looked headed for worse. I've warned about gold for a few months now. Can’t help anymore. 
- Enough of the pessimistic picture. WHERE do we look for opportunities? The sectors I mentioned in the 17 Jun post are still valid. Neoclouds. Cybersecurity. Biotech. Healthcare. 
- In the Neocloud space, NBIS WULF HUT etc all holding well. NBIS is still the leader. Yes some of them are also down slightly. But compared that to an AAOI, AXTI, AMD, ARM, etc and these Neoclouds will look like champs.
- In Cyber, FTNT, CRWD, PANW all looking good and when the market turns, these could potentially lead. 
- I am wary of buying individual biotech names because of the gap risk. Am expressing that view via ETFs. 
- All the best. Not advice. Risks involved.

What a rug pull. - The good news: this is not taking us by surprise. Whilst we took some paper cuts in the past 2 weeks, we've largely avoided getting caught in a big drawdown by holding a disproportionate amount of cash coming into this. - You may be wondering, why even have any paper cuts (small losses) at all? Because we CANNOT predict anything. We do not know WITH CERTAINTY whether the market will Up, Down or Sideways tomorrow or any time in the future. That means when we get good setups, we take the trade. But we manage our risk TIGHTLY at the same time so that if it turns out wrong, we don't lose a lot of money. But when it goes our way, we let it ride a little (eg our picks in biotech). - I will make a separate post about ZETA (which we bought today, in the midst of the selloff) as it will illustrate my thinking in more detail. - Guess what, technically the indices are still in an uptrend! But as I've said previously, the easy money is over. Even if the trend keeps going, it will be very volatile. I am a little encouraged that whilst the SPX is down 1.44% today, the equal weighted RSP is only down 0.34%. This tells me a lot of the selling has been led by mega caps. Which is no wonder. Remember our post yesterday? We talked about how the MAG7 is totally thrashed for now. - Similar picture, but more nasty in the Nasdaq, which is down 3.3% for the day. See the post last week on 17 Jun 2026 where I gave a high level overview of the key tech sectors like Semicon, Photonics etc. I found it hard to get heavily involved in most of the AI Hardware related names. And the market is "funny/ironic" like this. I suspect A LOT of latecomers and retail will be rushing into the AI hardware trade / or had rushed in a few weeks ago on the dip. Why? Because they were locked out since the 30 Mar bottom. And the first chance they get to buy on a pullback 1-2 weeks ago, a lot may have rushed in. Why ironic? Because this has usually been how the market worked. Sucker in a lot of latecomers at the top. - How do you prevent something like this? Situational Awareness. I can't do all this in one post, and there is a lot of nuance involved. Experience and having seen many market turns in the past counts too. - Right now, I am still NOT prepared to call this a market top. The trend is still up, although there is a very high chance we will at least go into choppy sideways action for a while. The SPX has now come up against its 50SMA support, and we have earnings season soon. Maybe that will keep the market supported. On the bear side, the SPX higher timeframe (Weekly) MOMENTUM is threatening to cut / has nicked Down. This will imply the current lower timeframe (Daily) has turned DOWN. - Nothing much to say about BTC and Gold, both looked headed for worse. I've warned about gold for a few months now. Can’t help anymore. - Enough of the pessimistic picture. WHERE do we look for opportunities? The sectors I mentioned in the 17 Jun post are still valid. Neoclouds. Cybersecurity. Biotech. Healthcare. - In the Neocloud space, NBIS WULF HUT etc all holding well. NBIS is still the leader. Yes some of them are also down slightly. But compared that to an AAOI, AXTI, AMD, ARM, etc and these Neoclouds will look like champs. - In Cyber, FTNT, CRWD, PANW all looking good and when the market turns, these could potentially lead. - I am wary of buying individual biotech names because of the gap risk. Am expressing that view via ETFs. - All the best. Not advice. text:
Quantum technology is moving fast and the labs behind it need relocation partners who understand what is at stake.

Quantum computing, quantum physics, cryogenic research, photonics, sensing, and advanced materials labs are not standard moves. These environments often include dilution refrigerators, optical tables, lasers, vacuum systems, cryogenic equipment, high-value electronics, custom-built instruments, and years of active research.

At USA Lab Movers, we specialize in moving complex scientific environments with the planning, custom crating, rigging, trucking, cold-chain support, and on-site project management these projects require.

The goal is simple: protect the equipment, reduce downtime, and help researchers get back to work as quickly and safely as possible.

Read our latest article on moving quantum computing, quantum physics, and advanced research labs.

Quantum computing, quantum physics, cryogenic research, photonics, sensing, and advanced materials labs are not standard moves. These environments often include dilution refrigerators, optical tables, lasers, vacuum systems, cryogenic equipment, high-value electronics, custom-built instruments, and years of active research. At USA Lab Movers, we specialize in moving complex scientific environments with the planning, custom crating, rigging, trucking, cold-chain support, and on-site project management these projects require. The goal is simple: protect the equipment, reduce downtime, and help researchers get back to work as quickly and safely as possible. text:
Sivers Semi ($SIVE) is presenting a strong buying opportunity on this pullback.

The core fundamentals remain fully intact. The company is well-positioned in AI data center optics and photonics with a solid pipeline, growing demand for their laser and interconnect tech, and real execution potential ahead.

The recent downside appears driven largely by short seller pressure and manipulation — high short interest that’s disconnected from the underlying business strength.

This is a dip worth considering for those focused on AI infrastructure. Long-term setup looks compelling.

Anyone else buying today?

The disconnect here is purely structural: local European markets often fail to price deep-tech optionality correctly, misinterpreting strategic capital expansion as structural weakness.

For $SIVE, migrating to NASDAQ isn't just about liquidity; it’s about shifting the valuation to institutional investors who actually understand upstream photonics architecture and 60% GM profiles.

text:
Bears most of the time actually don't misunderstand. 

They reframe it to fill their egos on why they are on calling out stocks like $SIVE and $AAOI not being good buys today.

It is so funny how most of the market goes against these names when things are down due to macro conditions, with nothing related actually to the fundamentals/thesis of such companies. 

InP laser shortage is going to kick in next year. 1.6T/CPO timelines will keep compressing.

Obviously the gross margins and revenue growth will show on the balance sheet of these photonic companies. Afterall, it is the next bull cycle of the overall AI buildout.

Everyone betted against $MU, $SNDK last year. Now they are the so called bulls that called it early. 2026 is showing memory shortage.

A lot of investments & money is going into delivering when ramps start to scale in 2027. This was confirmed by earnings from $LITE, $COHR, $AAOI, and many others. 

BoFA and GS reports back this well. They aren't your cheap X account researches that throw out rubbish. These are institutions with a big ecosystem connection to the overall technological industry, including AI companies. Analysts, experts and technicians in the field write these reports that take weeks (maybe months), not just a few days of research. 

After all being said and research by @aleabitoreddit and all others, if one still doesn't believe in the story playing out and why this is true, I think it would be good to reconsider and have a check in the mirror if you are just a soft hand in the market with absolutely zero real conviction wherever you invest. Today it could be photonics. Tomorrow you'll find another one. 

Some habits don't change. It requires a change in the overall mindset and character of the investor. That is why I always reinforce the need to construct your own system, and not blindly follow anybody. Even me. 

- AI

text:
Who is Coherent ( $COHR )? 

Coherent is a leader in photonics and laser technology, supplying high-speed optical components used in AI data centers and advanced networking.

NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang recently broke ground on Coherent’s new U.S. laser factory, showing that NVIDIA is securing optics supply early the same strategy it used with HBM and advanced packaging.

Financials & Outlook
• Datacom segment showing solid growth driven by AI demand
• Focus on margin expansion while building next-gen photonics capacity
• AI optics viewed as a key multi-year growth driver into 2026–2027

What Separates Coherent
• Broader portfolio than pure transceiver plays like $AAOI 
• Deeper vertical integration (laser chips to modules) vs. $LITE
• Direct $NVDA partnership on domestic manufacturing adds strategic weight

The recent pullback has created a small entry point on a name with growing importance in the AI supply chain. 

Let me know what you guys think about this one has it ran its course or is it still a buy ?

And it's just a matter of how much Win Semi + others partners can make, with $SIVE receiving ~60% gross margins + optionality to TAM expand downward. The 15% recent share expansion that Swedish media have been bearposting is for NASDAQ listing float + M&A (and it's authorization). You just have potential short sellers running illegal bot farms, Swedish media bearposting a company Swedish exchange, on top of a general macro drop. The ~$140M convertible note is real, but it's pennies to US institutions. We'll likely see more institutional ownership when data comes out. But this is also why it's good for $SIVE to prioritize NASDAQ listing so they don't need to deal with this noise and local media. With $AAOI at $10B, you have: - A end-to-end US laser/design/assembly player projecting $471m month revenue H1 entering H2 2027. - With other hyperscalers like $AMD apparently discussing LTAs. And they probably are sitting on a ton of cash after running ~$1.4B in ATMs (400M + 400m+ 600m). Kinda all that needs to be said with those insane revenue projections as long as management doesn't BS. This just reminds me of when $NBIS crashed to $70 last year even while projecting $7-9B ARR, and as timelines got closer it recovered to $250+. Feels like dejavu. I'm just waiting for both volume ramps to hit. text: @Reuters safran picking up exail consolidates european photonics and navigation under one roof, strategically smart move

$INFQ recent contract / funding momentum:

U.S. Navy : $1M contract for QuIRC, Infleqtion’s quantum-inspired AI software for RF signal processing in contested environments.
DARPA HARQ: $2M contract to develop Multistaq, a software platform for heterogeneous quantum systems across multiple qubit types.
NASA JPL: $20M in contracts to date for the Quantum Gravity Gradiometer Pathfinder mission, where Infleqtion is helping build space-based quantum sensing tech.
U.S. Department of Commerce / CHIPS Office: proposed $100M funding LOI to accelerate U.S. neutral-atom quantum computing, quantum hardware, photonics, and full-stack system development.
Important distinction: the $100M CHIPS item is not a finalized contract yet, but it is proposed, milestone-based funding subject to final documents and approvals. 

My Friday brain says: small announced contract dollars today, but strong validation from Navy, DARPA, NASA, DOE, and Commerce. $INFQ is building government credibility in quantum computing, sensing, defense, and space.
You know I love me some space.

text:
$IONQ

A recent report by Kearney makes it clear that trapped ions are the most promising way of building commercially useful quantum computers.

The report discusses these architectures:

1/ Trapped ions

Highest fidelity but slower gate speeds

All-to-all connectivity leads to lower physical qubit overhead

Best from unit-economics perspective

Bottom line: It’s the most commercially viable quantum computers. You’ll get high quality data when using them but have to wait for your calculations longer.

2/ Superconducting qubits

Fast gate times

Higher physical qubit overhead than for trapped ions

But even under optimistic assumptions, scaling will make system costs unsustainable

Report actually calls this an “existential architectural threat”

Bottom line: There’s a good chance you’ll never see a useful quantum computer built on millions of superconducting qubits

3/ Neutral atoms

High fidelity

All-to-all connectivity leading to lower physical qubit overhead like for trapped ions

As promising as trapped-ion technology but real challenge is engineering: Calibration automation, system resets, and error correction

Bottom line: Definitely has potential to scale up but not clear how engineering challenges will be overcome

4/ Photonics

Combines two advantages: Room-temperature operation and compatibility with foundries currently building semiconductor chips

Biggest bet is modularity, which means you break quantum computers into small interconnected processing units

Biggest challenge is scaling because photons don’t naturally interact with each other and light is subject to losses

Bottom line: Not clear if this manufacturing leverage will help scale photonics.

The conclusion of the report speaks for itself:

“The architectures most likely to lead are therefore the ones that can repeatedly translate physics progress into operationally stable, cost-improving systems, because the market will ultimately reward not “qubits on a slide,” but sustained improvement in error-corrected capability per dollar.

Based on Kearney analysis in collaboration with leading quantum companies, industry and academic experts, trapped-ion quantum computers are early leaders to be the most cost-effective quantum computer at fault tolerant scale.”

Long $IONQ.

“… the tape is treating a triple-digit grower in the middle of the photonics boom like the thesis fell apart, and it did not. The orders are signed, and the factories are going up.

Everyone got scared right as it got cheap.”

$AAOI
embedded_text:
AAOI is trading at about 2x what the company itself expects to do in 2027. For a stock growing triple digits, that looks pretty rich.

The whole photonics group got hit the last couple of weeks, and almost none of it was about the companies. Co-packaged optics delay scares https://t.co/x9DZoQTDoy

For a stock growing triple digits, that looks pretty rich. The whole photonics group got hit the last couple of weeks, and almost none of it was about the companies. Co-packaged optics delay scares spooked people, the Fed turned hawkish, the memory names cracked, and the high-beta optical stocks got dumped with everything else. Classic crowded trade unwinding. Nothing actually changed in the businesses. So look at what you are getting at $130. The market cap is around $10.6 billion. Management's own target for mid-2027 is about $471 million a month in data center revenue, or roughly $5.6 billion a year. That puts the stock at about 2x that target today. Slower photonics names trade richer than that on the numbers they have already done. And this is not a hope and a prayer. Full-year guidance is $1.1 billion, more than double last year. Q1 grew 51%. They have over $324 million in locked 800G and 1.6T orders. They are expanding laser capacity by around 350% by the end of 2027. Supply is expected to trail demand the whole way through. They start shipping their big 1.6T order on July 1. Rosenblatt sees $220. The one fair knock is that management has to actually hit the numbers, and they have been lumpy in the past. That is the real risk, and I am not going to pretend it away. But the tape is treating a triple-digit grower in the middle of the photonics boom like the thesis fell apart, and it did not. The orders are signed, and the factories are going up. Everyone got scared right as it got cheap. text:
Quantum Computing Inc. $QUBT just made one of its biggest strategic moves—and it’s about far more than buying another company.

The acquisition of NHanced Semiconductors signals that QUBT is shifting from being a company with promising quantum technology to one that wants control over manufacturing and commercialization.

That difference matters.

Many quantum companies have brilliant science but depend on third-party manufacturers to turn prototypes into real products. QUBT is trying to shorten that gap.

The deal is structured intelligently.

• $68.1M cash upfront
• $5M in QUBT stock
• Up to $72M in earnout payments only if NHanced achieves ambitious revenue and EBITDA targets during 2027-2028.
• $20M remains in escrow, protecting QUBT if performance milestones aren’t reached.

This isn’t simply buying technology.

It’s buying execution, while making sellers earn the highest valuation through future performance.

For long-term investors, that’s a positive signal.

NHanced brings semiconductor packaging, advanced integration, and manufacturing capabilities that could become critical as QUBT develops photonic quantum computing, quantum sensing, AI hardware, and integrated optical chips.

The bigger picture is even more interesting.

The future AI stack won’t just require better software—it will require new hardware.

Quantum computing, silicon photonics, optical interconnects, advanced semiconductor packaging, and AI accelerators are increasingly converging into one ecosystem.

Owning more of that value chain gives QUBT greater control over product quality, production timelines, margins, intellectual property, and customer relationships.

However, investors should remain realistic.

The acquisition alone doesn’t prove commercial success.

QUBT still needs to demonstrate that NHanced can generate meaningful revenue growth and that the combined business can manufacture products at scale.

The required financial statements and pro-forma results have not yet been released, so it’s still too early to fully judge whether the purchase price represents a bargain or an expensive bet.

Meanwhile, the recent criticism surrounding Microsoft’s topological quantum research serves as another reminder of how early this industry still is.

Scientific breakthroughs remain uncertain.

Engineering remains difficult.

Commercial adoption will likely take years.

That’s why companies with real manufacturing capabilities may eventually separate themselves from companies relying solely on research papers.

The next phase of quantum computing won’t be won by the company with the best theory.

It will likely be won by the company that can design, manufacture, scale, and deliver commercially viable quantum hardware.

QUBT is betting that controlling more of its manufacturing pipeline today will give it a stronger competitive position tomorrow.

text:
The biggest investing mistake?
Thinking the next decade will look like the last one.
While most investors chase quarterly headlines, the biggest wealth creators are often built around long-term themes that play out over years:
📷 AI Infrastructure
📷 Space
📷 Robotics
📷 Photonics
📷 Energy
📷 Quantum Computing
📷 Rare Earths
📷 Defense Technology
📷 Genomics
These aren’t just industries.
They’re some of the technologies reshaping how we work, communicate, travel, defend, power, and improve our world.
The companies leading these trends today may become the market leaders of tomorrow.
The question isn’t whether change is coming.
The question is: Which trends will create the most value over the next 10 years?
Which mega theme are you most bullish on? 📷
#Investing #GrowthStocks #StockMarket #FutureTech #LongTermInvesting

text:
Millimeter-wave (mmWave) technology is powering the next generation of ultra-fast wireless communication. Discover innovative antennas, electro-optic modulators, and key components driving 5G and beyond. 

https://t.co/TIc1uCneZN

#5G #mmWave #Photonics #Wireless #Telecom https://t.co/pZV8Uzeg9y

$IQE
One of the most important points from FY2025, in my view, was that IQE is seeing increasing customer demand for 6/8-inch GaSb-based epiwafers, while its LWIR product has achieved production qualification with a Tier 1 customer.

I believe infrared-based LWIR/MWIR, and potentially SWIR as well, could become a very important growth pillar for IQE through 2030.

IQE’s sensing portfolio can broadly be divided into four platforms:

GaAs: VCSEL / 3D sensing
InP: SWIR / photonics
GaN: LiDAR drivers / microLED
GaSb: IR detectors / T2SL / MWIR / LWIR

In other words, IQE is a rare pure-play merchant epitaxy foundry that owns all the key III-V platforms: GaAs, InP, GaN, and GaSb.

Among these, the area I am particularly focused on is GaSb-based MWIR/LWIR.

Robotics, drones, autonomous driving, and defence systems are still in the early stages of growth.

Today, many systems combine RGB cameras, LiDAR, radar, ToF, and other sensors, but as these systems become more advanced, sensing will not end with RGB cameras alone.

RGB cameras are extremely powerful in the visible spectrum.

They are excellent for recognizing colour, shape, text, and surface-level information.

However, visible-light-based data has clear limitations.

At night, in smoke, dust, fog, strong backlight, camouflage, and adverse weather, RGB cameras alone cannot provide sufficiently robust information.

Future robots, drones, and unmanned systems will not simply need to “see” what is visible.

They will also need to understand the thermal information emitted by people, vehicles, engines, batteries, and industrial equipment.

This is where MWIR/LWIR becomes increasingly important.

MWIR is linked to high-temperature objects, engines, missiles, long-range surveillance, aerospace, and defence systems.

LWIR is linked to people, animals, machine heat, night surveillance, thermal imaging, and hazardous-environment perception.

In other words, MWIR/LWIR may not merely be a “supporting sensor” for future autonomous systems.

In certain environments, it could become a core perception layer that is even more important than RGB.

This is especially true in defence.

The future battlefield is moving toward drones, unmanned vehicles, surveillance systems, automatic target recognition, and night operations.

In these environments, high-resolution RGB images alone are not enough.

To detect people in darkness, vehicles with residual engine heat, hidden equipment, missile-launch heat signatures, or abnormal machine heat, MWIR/LWIR thermal sensing is required.

The same logic applies to robotics.

If humanoids or industrial robots only operate in bright indoor environments, RGB + depth sensors may be enough.

But once they expand into dark factories, warehouses, night patrol, disaster sites, military robotics, and machine overheating detection, the story changes.

For robots, MWIR/LWIR can become both “eyes that see at night” and a “sense that detects heat.”

The problem is that existing MWIR/LWIR sensors have been very expensive.

High-performance IR detectors have been costly because of small substrates, complex epitaxy, low yields, ROIC hybrid bonding, cryocoolers, and expensive optics.

As a result, they have mainly been used in markets that can absorb the cost, such as defence, aerospace, and specialised industrial applications.

But IQE’s 6/8-inch GaSb-based epiwafer directly targets one of these bottlenecks.

When wafer size increases, it is not simply about making the wafer bigger.

The number of dies per wafer increases, large-area manufacturing productivity improves, and there is room for better uniformity and yield.

This creates the foundation for producing high resolution FPAs focal plane arrays more economically.

Simply put, high performance thermal sensing, which was previously too expensive and limited mainly to defence and specialised applications, may have the potential to move into broader markets through large diameter epi platforms.

IQE is not talking about this as a simple R&D project.

It has mentioned both Tier 1 production qualification for its LWIR product and increasing demand for 6/8-inch GaSb-based epiwafers.

As humanoid physical AI models mature and need more sensing vectors to become robust against real-world noise, I believe there will come a point where additional modalities become necessary.

In autonomous driving, this could happen even earlier, with MWIR/LWIR potentially being integrated sooner.

The fact that production qualification has already been completed with a Tier 1 customer suggests that this demand is not theoretical anymore. It may already be starting.

Beyond data centres, how do you view IQE’s future in robotics and autonomous driving?

text:
خلال السنوات القليلة الماضية شهدت الأسواق انتقالات متسارعة لرأس المال بين موجات تكنولوجية متعاقبة.

بدأت الموجة مع السيارات الكهربائية والطاقة النظيفة ثم انتقل التركيز إلى الذكاء الاصطناعي تلاه الاهتمام بالحوسبة الكمية ثم البنية التحتية لمراكز البيانات والطاقة اللازمة لدعم الاقتصاد الرقمي الجديد.

لكن تاريخ الابتكار يشير إلى أن كل ثورة تقنية تخلق احتياجات جديدة وتمهد الطريق للثورة التالية.

وفي تقديري فإن المرحلة القادمة قد تتمحور حول التقنيات التي تدمج الذكاء الاصطناعي بالعالم المادي والإنسان نفسه.

الروبوتات البشرية وواجهات الدماغ والحاسوب والحوسبة الضوئية والتكنولوجيا الحيوية المدعومة بالذكاء الاصطناعي قد تمثل الركائز الأساسية للعقد القادم.

السؤال الذي يجب أن يطرحه المستثمرون ليس ما هو القطاع الأكثر زخماً اليوم؟
بل ما هي التقنيات التي ستعيد تشكيل الاقتصاد العالمي خلال السنوات العشر القادمة؟

فالأسواق لا تكافئ الإجماع.

إنها تكافئ الرؤية المبكرة.

text:
Die Verlagerung der Halbleitersicherungsqualität von Siliziumkarbid hin zur optischen KI Infrastruktur

Der explosive Anstieg der Rechenlasten in modernen Rechenzentren verändert die Anforderungen an die physische Zuverlässigkeit von Mikrochips grundlegend. Da der Ausfall einzelner Komponenten in massiven KI Clustern immense finanzielle Schäden verursacht, rücken spezialisierte Verfahren zur Qualitätsprüfung direkt auf Wafer-Ebene ins Zentrum der globalen Wertschöpfungskette.
Das US Halbleiterprüfunternehmen Aehr Test Systems demonstriert diese transformationale Dynamik in der ersten Hälfte des Jahres 2026 durch einen beispiellosen Kursanstieg an den Finanzmärkten. Die Aktie, die zu Beginn des Jahres noch nahe der 20 US Dollar Marke notierte, kletterte im Juni 2026 zeitweise auf über 115 US Dollar. Dieser Zuwachs spiegelt den erfolgreichen strategischen Wandel des Unternehmens wider. Während Aehr Test Systems in den vergangenen Jahren primär von Systemen für Siliziumkarbid-Leistungshalbleiter im stagnierenden Elektromobilitätssektor abhing, positioniert sich der Konzern nun als kritischer Infrastrukturpartner für die optische Datenübertragung in KI Rechenzentren.
Den technologischen Hebel bildet die proprietäre FOX-XP Plattform, ein vollautomatisiertes System für das sogenannte Wafer-Level Burn-In. Bei diesem Verfahren werden hunderte Halbleiter-Wafer gleichzeitig unter extremen thermischen und elektrischen Bedingungen künstlich gealtert, um fehlerhafte Bauteile noch vor dem finalen Verpackungsprozess zu identifizieren. Ein entscheidender Engpass moderner Beschleuniger-Cluster liegt in der Kommunikation über optische Transceiver (Silicon Photonics), die Lichtsignale statt elektrischer Impulse nutzen. Da diese optischen Schnittstellen extrem anfällig für frühe Ausfälle sind, verlangen führende Hyperscaler eine lückenlose Vorabprüfung.
Die finanzielle Bestätigung dieser Neuausrichtung lieferten die jüngsten Auftragseingänge. Nach einem vorangegangenen, operativ schwächeren Quartal mit lediglich 10,3 Millionen US Dollar Umsatz meldete Aehr für die zweite Jahreshälfte 2026 einen massiven Sprung bei den Buchungen auf über 92 Millionen US Dollar. Treiber war eine Serie von Großaufträgen, darunter eine Rekordbestellung über 41 Millionen US Dollar von einem führenden Hyperscale-KI Kunden sowie Folgeaufträge eines globalen Marktführers für Netzwerklösungen. Diese Systeme dienen sowohl der technologischen Qualifikation neuer Chip-Designs als auch der anschließenden Hochvolumenproduktion.
Trotz dieser operativen Meilensteine bleibt die Aktie an den Kapitalmärkten von hoher Volatilität geprägt, was sich in einer temporären Korrektur Ende Juni auf rund 91 US Dollar äußerte. Mit einem Kurs-Umsatz-Verhältnis von über 60 reflektiert die Bewertung bereits erhebliche Vorschusslorbeeren auf die kommenden Fiskaljahre. Da das Unternehmen den kapitalintensiven Hochlauf zudem über ein aktives Aktienplatzierungsprogramm (At-The-Market) mitfinanziert, müssen die anstehenden Quartalszahlen eine fehlerfreie Skalierung der Auslieferungen belegen. Für den Technologiesektor untermauert der Fall Aehr jedoch ein fundamentales Prinzip: Der wirtschaftliche Erfolg im KI Zyklus verschiebt sich zunehmend von den reinen Chip-Designern hin zu den hochspezialisierten Enablern, welche die physische Betriebssicherheit des Gesamtsystems garantieren.

Da der Ausfall einzelner Komponenten in massiven KI Clustern immense finanzielle Schäden verursacht, rücken spezialisierte Verfahren zur Qualitätsprüfung direkt auf Wafer-Ebene ins Zentrum der globalen Wertschöpfungskette. Das US Halbleiterprüfunternehmen Aehr Test Systems demonstriert diese transformationale Dynamik in der ersten Hälfte des Jahres 2026 durch einen beispiellosen Kursanstieg an den Finanzmärkten. Die Aktie, die zu Beginn des Jahres noch nahe der 20 US Dollar Marke notierte, kletterte im Juni 2026 zeitweise auf über 115 US Dollar. Dieser Zuwachs spiegelt den erfolgreichen strategischen Wandel des Unternehmens wider. Während Aehr Test Systems in den vergangenen Jahren primär von Systemen für Siliziumkarbid-Leistungshalbleiter im stagnierenden Elektromobilitätssektor abhing, positioniert sich der Konzern nun als kritischer Infrastrukturpartner für die optische Datenübertragung in KI Rechenzentren. Den technologischen Hebel bildet die proprietäre FOX-XP Plattform, ein vollautomatisiertes System für das sogenannte Wafer-Level Burn-In. Bei diesem Verfahren werden hunderte Halbleiter-Wafer gleichzeitig unter extremen thermischen und elektrischen Bedingungen künstlich gealtert, um fehlerhafte Bauteile noch vor dem finalen Verpackungsprozess zu identifizieren. Ein entscheidender Engpass moderner Beschleuniger-Cluster liegt in der Kommunikation über optische Transceiver (Silicon Photonics), die Lichtsignale statt elektrischer Impulse nutzen. Da diese optischen Schnittstellen extrem anfällig für frühe Ausfälle sind, verlangen führende Hyperscaler eine lückenlose Vorabprüfung. Die finanzielle Bestätigung dieser Neuausrichtung lieferten die jüngsten Auftragseingänge. Nach einem vorangegangenen, operativ schwächeren Quartal mit lediglich 10,3 Millionen US Dollar Umsatz meldete Aehr für die zweite Jahreshälfte 2026 einen massiven Sprung bei den Buchungen auf über 92 Millionen US Dollar. Treiber war eine Serie von Großaufträgen, darunter eine Rekordbestellung über 41 Millionen US Dollar von einem führenden Hyperscale-KI Kunden sowie Folgeaufträge eines globalen Marktführers für Netzwerklösungen. Diese Systeme dienen sowohl der technologischen Qualifikation neuer Chip-Designs als auch der anschließenden Hochvolumenproduktion. text:
Disorder creates new properties in compound semiconductors - Helmholtz-Zentrum Berlin (HZB) https://t.co/YcmkNFjBWE

This first clear demonstration of phonon-exciton asymmetry opens doors to next-gen optoelectronics. The material pretends to be a bland cubic citizen but reveals a sophisticated, directionally picky inner life under light. Revolutionary for energy tech and photonics, disorder as deliberate design tool. Schorr’s team calls it “an additional handle for tailoring optoelectronic properties.”

Talk about a stick save  $LITE 

You got that potential U&R (Undercut and Recover) on the 100 day for 

Should be an interesting rest of week for this name and many other photonics names ex $GLW which is living in its own world.  

Have they shook out all the new comers and they are ready for a new leg?  Or is this Lucy waving the football at Charlie Brown just to pull it away. 

No one knows, but I'll have the 🍿 out

text:
8/11

This is where silicon photonics and micro-ring resonators become important.

A micro-ring can act like a tiny wavelength-selective filter.

It can help select, modulate, or separate specific wavelengths.

That makes WDM easier to integrate into photonic chips.

text:
This is the right take → attention relative to float is a fragility gauge, not a buy signal. The crowding z-score peaks right before the air pocket.

What's worth adding → it's symmetric, and your own example is confirming it in real time. The radar caught flow rotating OUT of photonics. Photonics is bouncing today. The same sensor that flagged crowding fragility on the way up flags abandonment on the way down → attention decays, the float stays pinned, and the setup reloads.

Crowding fragile, neglect cheap, same indicator read in two directions. Your Burry-SOXX fun fact is the purest version of that → peak query is peak neglect is the bottom. 👀

text:
$SIVE and $AAPL. Funny how a company named Rockley led us here.

A lot of you are asking if this connection is confirmed. Let me break it down.

Rockley Photonics was Apple's laser supplier for their glucose monitoring project. Apple poured $70 million into them.

Rockley went bankrupt in 2023.

So where does $AAPL go next?

Sivers Semiconductors is a small Swedish company that makes extremely precise lasers. So precise that they are the only laser technology in the world that can measure blood glucose through your skin without a needle.

That is not marketing. That is physics.

Here is what is officially confirmed.

In January 2024 $SIVE published a legal regulatory filing. The kind where executives face criminal charges if they lie.

It states that a US Fortune 100 company has been working with $SIVE for over 5 years. Paid $17 million in development fees. And just placed an order for 50,000 laser devices for a wearable health product.

That is real. That is in the filing.

The timing lines up. The technology fits. The unit volumes match.

$AAPL is never named in any $SIVE document. That part is still inference.

But if it is confirmed casually run these numbers.
Apple Watch ships up to 54 million units a year. At $10 to $15 per laser that is $500 to $750 million in potential annual revenue for $SIVE. Comparable sole source Apple suppliers have historically traded at 25 to 40 times sales at peak.

$SIVE sits at a $1.9 billion market cap today.
Rockley bankruptcy. $AAPL needs a new laser supplier. $SIVE announces a Fortune 100 wearable customer.

The confirmed volume order is the catalyst that changes everything.

text: Nokia/Infinera is a real position in the optical stack — but the market is still pricing it as a telecom story. The deeper opportunity is in silicon photonics and electro-optic modulators that eliminate the electrical-to-optical conversion bottleneck inside AI clusters entirely. Analyst downgrades miss the thesis shift.

text:
Funny how the market spent weeks arguing over $AXTI, $LITE and $IQE, while $ONTO quietly moved from about $287 to $345.

That is roughly a 20% move since I called it out, but the price move is not the main point.

The cleaner thesis was never “buy another photonics stock.” It was that co-packaged optics changes the cost of failure. In the old pluggable world, a bad part could be discarded. In CPO, a defect found after bonding can destroy a far more valuable assembly.

That pushes inspection upstream. It makes pre-bond metrology a gating step, not a support function.

So if $TSM, $NVDA and $AVGO are pushing the industry toward advanced optical packaging, the question is not only who supplies the light. It is who helps protect yield before the package becomes too expensive to fail.

That is why $ONTO mattered.

Not the flashiest name in photonics. Just one of the harder steps to bypass.

The stock moved first. The manufacturing constraint is still there.

"text": "While everyone is fighting over $AXTI, $LITE, and $IQE… There’s a company quietly sitting at a chokepoint that NONE of them can function without. $ONTO (Onto Innovation) — ~$14B market cap Here’s why this is different from every other photonics stock people are talking about. In the old pluggable world, if a chip failed during assembly, you threw away a $50 part. In the new CPO (Co-Packaged Optics) world — where light is fused permanently into the silicon — a single defect discovered AFTER bonding means scrapping a $10,000 assembly. That changes everything. Every CPO chip now has to be inspected at the wafer level BEFORE it ever gets permanently bonded. There is no bypass. There is no exemption. $ONTO’s Dragonfly G5 is the system doing that inspection. And in April 2026, it was officially qualified as the foundry standard for 2.5D advanced packaging. Think about what that means: - $TSMC runs COUPE (the CPO platform $NVDA and $AVGO are built on) - Every chip going into that platform needs pre-bonding optical inspection - The tool of record for that inspection just became Dragonfly G5 - There is no credible alternative at volume scale This is the “yield insurance” that makes the entire Glass City buildable. Without $ONTO, CPO doesn’t scale. The numbers back it up: - Q1 2026 revenue: $292M — beat guidance - Q2 2026 guidance: $320–$330M — 8% raise - Full year 2026 revenue projected to grow 30%+ - Advanced packaging and AI segments leading growth - 53.8% gross margins - Dragonfly G5: shipped, qualified, ramping And FinTwit is still arguing about InP substrates. $AXTI and $LITE get the attention because the thesis is simple to explain. $ONTO gets ignored because “wafer inspection” doesn’t sound sexy. But it is the last gate every single CPO chip must pass through before it goes live. The entire photonics supercycle runs through this company. And barely anyone is talking about it. ----- *Not financial advice. Do your own due diligence."}}}, "rest_id": "2054190271466729642", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "3202", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2071659851273429306", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "3091", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
"AI data centers hit a new bottleneck, optical interconnects"

When their hypes are over, now their reality in AI chip supply chain. 

I don't need to be charted, if I was selected at my start.

Your investors should know : Manufacturing in testing layers means where optical interconnect scaling and silicon photonics capacity are emerging as key constraints on AI data centre buildout. 

Which aligns with this week's observation, where AI data centres handles the scaling in larger workloads, in sell-offs, would they trigger the physical infrastructure connecting chips inside those facilities has becoming a limiting factor. 

Don't stir round their bs, this is fact. 

"Optical interconnects, which use light rather than electrical signals to move data, increased to be seen as the solution to bandwidth and power constraints at scale."

You tell me how this shift towards? 
It's not a design challenge, but more of "manufacturing and testing challenge", such as equipment suppliers, test solution providers, contract manufacturers, all play roles in determining how quickly optical interconnects technology can react high-volume production.

You tested that before, I know, how's the sudden pull-off, as if capacity running out, did you find remedies, when the heat is up, more volumes to scale increases of demands creation for specialised equipment and test infrastructure across several companies, typically component availability could associate as near-term execution risks. New partnerships and equipment order announcements signal that the photonics manufacturing ecosystem is highly depend on, actively expanding t meet AI data centre demand. 

Revenue targeting, your thought on these, AI chip supply chain in focus : semiconductor equipment, process control, foundry services, specialty chip design, all these companies in your industry.

Metrics: 
- CapEx, < 80%, over 100%, high risk 
- Free cash flow, keep slipping, nope. 
- Cloud services revenue and profitability, matched? 
- Management's perception : continual and rising demands, now changed into the tone of "Capital efficiency" to "slow the tempo down". 

A whole rinse up, bubbles to all their fluffs. 
The rest that can be existed is actually running with the infrastructure, users centric, enterprise architecture, may I remind you? 

If their pimps are still sitting at RFPs levels, they can't let you know how I wrote here, what makes you think I looked at those since 2019? :D

That's why 2024 dropped, 2025, unbothered and pushed harder, now what, you want to shut who up?

For sure, shut yours up next if they don't know what "AI adoption" means! 

What compliance project are you sourcing out? 
What's your market like now? :D

There’s so much alpha out there.

Hate to say it but I think we’re going lower on a lot of these photonics/memory/semi names

text:
💡Montana is well-equipped with a supply chain for a 21st century economy💡

In collaboration with the @MontanaChamber, our Policy Director @TannerMAvery recently gave a presentation on what data centers mean for Montana.🖥️ Highlighting that Montana has abundant critical minerals, high-tech photonics, and chip-manufacturing, Tanner shares: “when I think about data centers, I don’t just think that we should attract data centers for the sake of data centers. I’m thinking that when we can attract parts of the supply chain, we can attract the entire supply chain.”

Check out this clip! 👇 Link to full video below🔗

text:
$XFAB upgraded to Outperform with a €12.8 PT by Bernstein — up from €5.

Bullish call. But I think it's still undershooting the real optionality here.

🧵

The obvious thesis:

Automotive recovery + SiC/power semi ramp. Classic reactive upgrade. Bernstein is pricing in the cycle, not the platform.

What the market isn't pricing in yet:

$XFAB is quietly building a silicon photonics foundry capability through PhotonixFAB — in partnership with NVIDIA and LIGENTEC for thin-film lithium niobate (TFLN) on SOI.

If that platform matures into HVM (high-volume manufacturing), the valuation conversation changes completely.

You're not talking about a specialty analog/mixed-signal foundry anymore.

You're talking about a photonics-capable specialty foundry at the intersection of AI infrastructure, optical interconnects, and next-gen sensing.

That's a different multiple entirely.

My take:
Bernstein's upgrade is right on direction, wrong on magnitude.

The PT reflects the cyclical recovery. It doesn't reflect what XFAB becomes if the photonics bet hits.

Still bullish. Watching closely.

$XFAB

text:
Bernstein just upgraded X-FAB to Outperform with a price target of €12.80. While the upgrade is a welcome acknowledgment, I believe it’s still fundamentally reactive—primarily focused on automotive recovery and the SiC/power semiconductor ramp-up.

​The real upside? The market has yet to fully price in their potential move to High-Volume Manufacturing $HVM. With their work in silicon photonics (PhotonixFAB/Nvidia) and TFLN on SOI (LIGENTEC), X-FAB is positioned for much more than just a cyclical recovery. 

Bullish. $XFAb

text:
Nvidia spent $6.5B on photonics in 8 weeks. Copper can't keep up with 100K-GPU clusters.

Feynman (2028) drops copper entirely - NVLink 8 runs on fiber. TSMC's CPO is in high-volume production. Besi: 10x power, 20x latency gains.

Here's the part nobody talks about: AI data centers need 36x more fiber than traditional racks. Cable lead times hit one year. Preform expansion takes 18-24 months.

The next AI bottleneck isn't chip supply. It's glass.

#SiliconPhotonics #Nvidia #AISupplyChain #CPO

text:
Serenity’s response is exactly the mindset that we should all have

And one that I fully share

He is replying to someone who asked if he had lost faith in the photonics sector because $SIVE and $AAOI are falling sharply

His answer is clear: quite the opposite

He states that he is more bullish than ever, precisely while prices are dropping

He sees these declines as an attractive buying opportunity

As I’ve been saying for a while, the fundamental thesis is the same or even stronger than before the pullback

He highlights $SIVE in particular, which has a market cap of around $1.9B

Viewing it as an excellent entry point for investors

Personally, I have opened nearly my full long term position in $SIVE

In short, Serenity conveys optimism and strong confidence in the long term potential of the photonics sector

Encouraging everyone not to panic over short-term volatility

This is the message I also want to share

And one that is especially necessary for those who are feeling fear or are heavily underwater after the recent losses

I remain extremely bullish on photonics and memory 

In particular $SIVE $AAOI and $MU

I'm actually more bullish than ever as prices go down. $SIVE at ~$1.9B MC you have: - $GFS reference laser - laser for Ayar and others in $NVDA NVLink ecosystem for CPO scale up - $POET and others for CPO scale out - $JBL and others for 1.6T+ pluggable optical transcivers - O-Net for ELS mass production So you have all these hyperscaler suppliers trying to create as much as possible with Sivers as the laser bottleneck... And it's just a matter of how much Win Semi + others partners can make, with $SIVE receiving ~60% gross margins + optionality to TAM expand downward. The 15% recent share expansion that Swedish media have been bearposting is for NASDAQ listing float + M&A (and it's authorization). You just have potential short sellers running illegal bot farms, Swedish media bearposting a company Swedish exchange, on top of a general macro drop. The ~$140M convertible note is real, but it's pennies to US institutions. We'll likely see more institutional ownership when data comes out. But this is also why it's good for $SIVE to prioritize NASDAQ listing so they don't need to deal with this noise and local media. With $AAOI at $10B, you have: - A end-to-end US laser/design/assembly player projecting $471m month revenue H1 entering H2 2027. - With other hyperscalers like $AMD apparently discussing LTAs. And they probably are sitting on a ton of cash after running ~$1.4B in ATMs (400M + 400m+ 600m). Kinda all that needs to be said with those insane revenue projections as long as management doesn't BS. This just reminds me of when $NBIS crashed to $70 last year even while projecting $7-9B ARR, and as timelines got closer it recovered to $250+. Feels like dejavu. I'm just waiting for both volume ramps to hit. Photonics are generally more volatile than the rest."}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2070163176751054854"}}, "rest_id": "2070179074048409662", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "826689", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2070194228752191675", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "48877", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Despite high expectations for annual recurring revenue, valuations saw sharp declines, only to rebound rapidly as key milestones approached. Photonics stocks are notoriously volatile, so such fluctuations are simply part of the industry landscape.
If you plan to increase your holdings in $SIVE or $AAOI , avoid buying everything at once. Buy in batches – buy 25-30% initially, and then add more after the price falls further or the upward trend is confirmed. Strategically averaging down your cost amidst the volatility is key.

text:
AI sector bloodbath
Most everything getting wrecked except memory names like $MU  holding up okay.
Photonics down 40%, space stocks -40% in a month, $PLTR ~35% YTD, software names like $CRM -40%, crypto getting crushed too.
Hawkish Fed talk + liquidations hitting the smaller stuff hard. Feels like an overshoot. AI buildout thesis is still alive long-term, but macro noise is loud right now.
You buying dips in the beaten names or staying defensive?

text:
I'm not sure who's expecting a massive re-rate down.

But It's not adding up to me either. It's not necessarily super obvious, and you're right that markets are waiting for confirmation. But I think the risk to reward is worth it on AAOI. 

I think all of these photonics names got way too much interest from retail traders. And when that happens, there tends to be violent shakeouts especially with Jane Street involved.

I think the sector is also taking a breather

text:
The wait is over—the 2026 OCP APAC Summit schedule is now live!

Get ready for two days of technical innovation, collaboration and the latest thinking from leaders across the open infrastructure ecosystem.

This year’s program features sessions spanning:
·      AI Clusters & AI Computing Continuum
·      Open Data Centers for AI
·      Chiplets & Advanced Packaging
·      Networking & Photonics
·      Advanced Cooling Technologies
·      Server, Storage & Memory
·      Future Technologies shaping tomorrow’s infrastructure

Whether you’re designing next-generation AI infrastructure, advancing open hardware, or driving innovation across the data center ecosystem, there’s something on the agenda for you.

And we’re not done yet… our keynote lineup will be announced next week! Stay tuned for some exciting news.

A special thank you to the IOWN Global Forum, the AI on Chip Taiwan Alliance and the Cloud Computing and IoT Association of Taiwan for partnering with us as a co-host to help bring together the industry’s leading innovators and technologists.

August 11–12, 2026
Taipei, Taiwan

Explore the full schedule and start planning your Summit experience today: https://t.co/y8MtSdmFhL

And register here: https://t.co/RHWIUZmXl4

#OCPAPAC26 #OpenCompute #AIInfrastructure #Datacenter #OpenHardware #AI #Networking #Photonics #Cooling #Chiplets #Taiwan #IOWN
